hero.subtitle: Embedded Systems & Communication Engineer
hero.passion: Passionate about defense, aeronautics and robotics.
hero.search: Looking for new technical challenges as an embedded systems engineer

about.title: About me:
about.p1: I am Thibault L'Héritier, an Embedded Systems Engineer. The projects I've led are mainly related to simulator development and critical systems integration within the defense aeronautics sector.
about.p2: My expertise lies at the intersection of hardware and software: I design both hardware architectures and software layers to create complete and coherent systems.
about.p3: Rigorous and methodical, my approach is based on reliability and mastery of time constraints. I focus on understanding the inner workings of equipment (reverse engineering) to develop robust solutions capable of interfacing with existing technologies.
about.vision_p1: Through this journey, I am seeking a position in the design and development of critical systems, allowing me to apply my dual hardware and software skills while continuing to take on complex technical challenges.
about.vision_p2: I wish to contribute to the development of high-performance technological solutions, whether they be simulators, test tools, or complete embedded architectures.
about.vision_p3_expanded: My core motivation lies in technological innovation and the methodical rigor of engineering. I have a particular interest in the aeronautics, automotive, and robotics sectors, where precision and reliability are paramount.

iff.desc: Simulation of the IFF subsystem to display tracks (planes, boats) detected by the aircraft in real tactical situations.
iff.h1: Display of tracks from a tactical simulation software
iff.h2: Integration of IFF modes identical to behavior of real subsystem
iff.h3: Maximum representation of tactical reality for training
iff.details: The IFF simulator mimics the subsystem that displays tracks (planes, boats, etc.) detected in the aircraft. We display tracks from a simulation software to recreate a real tactical situation. The project includes the integration of different modes representing identical behavior of the real subsystem for optimal training on the PFS platform.

ifpm.desc: Simulation of the IFPM subsystem with an HMI for failure injection (IFF, Radar, Comm) by the instructor.
ifpm.h1: Instructor interface for creating degraded situations
ifpm.h2: Signal injection to computer for failure display identical to real
ifpm.h3: High-fidelity training on the PFS platform
ifpm.details: The IFPM simulator mimics the power and failure management subsystem. Through a dedicated HMI, the instructor can select failures on various systems (IFF, Radar, Comm) to simulate a degraded tactical situation. The simulator sends signals to the on-board computer so it displays the failures as if they were real.

skills.title: Skills Matrix
skills.col1_s1_desc: Understanding of heterogeneous structures (ARM/FPGA) and PS/PL interactions on Xilinx Zynq.
skills.col1_s2_desc: Mastery of physical and protocol layers: Serial, Synchronous, Full-Duplex.
skills.col1_s3_desc: Principles of state centralization (IFPM) and Hardware-in-the-Loop simulation.
skills.col2_s1_desc: Programming in VHDL (FPGA) and C/C++ (Software/HMI) with hard real-time constraint management.
skills.col2_s2_desc: Analysis of undocumented systems using an oscilloscope to decode protocols and ensure digital spoofing.
skills.col2_s3_desc: Industrial project management (10 months) in autonomy and functional tutoring.
skills.col3_s1_desc: Methodical analysis capacity to respect critical time requirements (< 2.5 µs).
skills.col3_s2_desc: Technical curiosity and tenacity in the face of complex technological hurdles.
skills.col3_s3_desc: Ability to popularize technical concepts to collaborate with various trades.
